routing.guidance: Classifies the problem as a policy, legal, or implementation system.

boundary.guidance: Pulls in agencies, courts, implementation chains, appropriations, and coalition politics.

structure.guidance: Makes legal authority, enforcement capacity, coalition support, and implementation mechanics central.

scenarios.guidance: Favors pass/fail, enforcement, litigation, implementation delay, and coalition-fracture branches.

question_generation.guidance: Favors authority, enforceability, discretion, implementation, and judicial-risk questions.

evidence_planning.guidance: Prefers legal text, agency guidance, procedural timing, litigation, and enforcement evidence.

decision_logic.guidance: Shapes what action means: intervention feasibility, compliance planning, timing, and leverage points.

signal_translation.guidance: Signals should emphasize bill text changes, rulemaking, litigation, appropriations, and compliance behavior.

monitoring.guidance: Monitoring should track implementation chain progress, enforcement intensity, and coalition shifts.

render.guidance: Mainly affects domain vocabulary and examples.
